lede: A look at February 27 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Constantine the Great and Akseli Kokkonen, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Boris Nemtsov
events.desc: Russian politician Boris Nemtsov is assassinated in Moscow while out walking with his girlfriend.

events.title: Dominican Republic
events.desc: The Dominican Republic receives its first democratically elected president, Juan Bosch, since the end of the dictatorship led by Rafael Trujillo.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: Operation Biting launches its overnight raid on the German coastal radar station at Bruneval to retrieve a Würzburg installation.

events.title: Martin Kamen
events.desc: Martin Kamen and Sam Ruben discover carbon-14.

events.title: Flag of Japan
events.desc: The current flag of Japan is first adopted as the national flag for Japanese merchant ships.

events.title: Abraham Lincoln
events.desc: Abraham Lincoln makes a speech at Cooper Union in the city of New York that plays an important role in his election to the Presidency.

events.title: American Revolutionary War
events.desc: American Revolutionary War: The Battle of Moore's Creek Bridge in North Carolina breaks up a Loyalist militia.

events.title: Sweden
events.desc: Sweden and the Tsardom of Russia sign the Treaty of Stolbovo, ending the Ingrian War and shutting Russia out of the Baltic Sea.

events.title: Treaty of Berwick (1560)
events.desc: The Treaty of Berwick is signed by England and the Lords of the Congregation of Scotland, establishing the terms under which English armed forces were to be permitted in Scotland in order to expel occupying French troops.

births.name: Maggie Hassan
births.line: American politician, 81st Governor of New Hampshire

births.name: John Steinbeck
births.line: American journalist and author, Nobel Prize laureate

births.name: Kazimierz Sabbat
births.line: Polish soldier and politician, President of Poland

births.name: Roman Giertych
births.line: Polish lawyer and politician, Deputy Prime Minister of the Republic of Poland

deaths.text: Boris Nemtsov, Russian academic and politician, First Deputy Prime Minister of Russia
Ivan Rebroff, German vocalist of Russian descent with four and a half octave range
Konrad Lorenz, Austrian zoologist, ethologist, and ornithologist, Nobel Prize laureate
Ivan Pavlov, Russian physiologist and physician, Nobel Prize laureate

observances.text: Christian feast day: Gabriel of Our Lady of Sorrows
